diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden severe unilateral pelvic pain is a cardinal feature of tubal rupture.
Also causes sudden unilateral pelvic pain.
Referred shoulder-tip pain indicates diaphragmatic irritation by free intraperitoneal blood (haemoperitoneum).
Syncope and altered mental state reflect cerebral hypoperfusion from haemorrhagic shock.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abnormal dark vaginal spotting for several days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Decidual breakdown produces abnormal, often dark, per-vaginal bleeding in ectopic pregnancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Amenorrhoea / missed or late menstrual period

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A late/missed period signals possible pregnancy — the pivotal information that reframes the presentation as a pregnancy-related emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound pallor with cold, clammy, sweaty skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral shutdown from haemorrhagic shock — caregiver-observable, admissible in telehealth as an unverified report.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distended abdomen with tenderness on approach

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Abdominal distension from haemoperitoneum; the caregiver can observe swelling and flinching but not elicit true guarding.
Objective decompensated haemorrhagic shock — a sealed clinician/device measurement; the AI infers shock from pallor, syncope and cold clammy skin, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distended rigid abdomen with generalised tenderness and rebound guarding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peritonism from haemoperitoneum — requires clinician palpation; sealed as answer key.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Pregnancy not confirmed; ectopic not distinguished from non-pregnant causes of haemoperitoneum.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive hCG establishes pregnancy; with an empty uterus and free fluid it confirms ectopic — hospital investigation, never available in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transvaginal ultrasound — free pelvic fluid and complex adnexal mass

investigation_details.expected_result: Massive free pelvic fluid (haemoperitoneum) with a complex adnexal mass; empty uterine cavity
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Site of bleeding not localised; delays surgical decision.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Free fluid + adnexal mass + empty uterus with a positive hCG is diagnostic of ectopic pregnancy — sealed hospital investigation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preceding high fever or purulent vaginal discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe pelvic sepsis secondary to pelvic inflammatory disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of preceding fever, discharge or gradual deterioration argues against a septic cause and favours an acute haemorrhagic event.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden unilateral pelvic pain + shoulder-tip pain + syncope + amenorrhoea together are near-diagnostic of a ruptured tubal ectopic with haemoperitoneum.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: This is the only appropriate 'investigation/action' from telehealth — definitive assessment (hCG, ultrasound, resuscitation, surgery) happens in hospital.
Confirms pregnancy and, with imaging, localises the ectopic.
Demonstrates haemoperitoneum and the adnexal mass; guides surgery.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Attempting remote vital-sign measurement or asking the collapsing patient to self-triage
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Wastes time in a peri-arrest emergency; escalation must not be delayed to gather data the AI cannot obtain.
